metadata.reason: Add literature-backed edges to bridge composite/component gaps exposed by the 15 pleiotropic cross-phenotype tests.

edges_added.mechanism: Cytokinin receptor histidine kinase composite (AHK2/AHK3/AHK4) activates B-type ARR transcription factors via AHP phosphorelay.

evidence.title: Histidine Kinase Homologs That Act as Cytokinin Receptors Possess Overlapping Functions in the Regulation of Shoot and Root Growth in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: ahk2 ahk3 double mutants exhibited a semidwarf phenotype as to shoots, such as a reduced leaf size and a reduced inflorescence stem length, and AHK receptors activate the AHP-ARR phosphorelay downstream.
evidence.claim: AHK cytokinin receptors activate B-type ARR (incl. ARR1) to regulate shoot growth; ahk2 ahk3 is a semidwarf.

edges_added.mechanism: EIN3/EIL1 recruit the FLD histone demethylase to the FLC locus, reducing H3K4me2 and repressing FLC transcription.

evidence.title: ETHYLENE INSENSITIVE3/EIN3-LIKE1 modulate FLOWERING LOCUS C expression via histone demethylase interaction
evidence.evidence_sentence: The ein3 eil1 double mutant had higher levels of H3K4me2 at the FLC locus and exhibited a late flowering phenotype, suggesting EIN3/EIL1 recruit FLD demethylase to repress FLC.
evidence.claim: EIN3 represses FLC; ein2/ein3 loss-of-function causes late flowering via sustained FLC expression.

edges_added.mechanism: Auxin-induced expression of LBD16/LBD29 drives lateral root founder cell specification and primordium initiation downstream of ARF7/ARF19.

evidence.title: ARF7 and ARF19 Regulate Lateral Root Formation via Direct Activation of LBD/ASL Genes in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: Auxin induces the expression of LBD16 and LBD29 through ARF7 and ARF19; lbd16 lbd29 double mutants show drastically reduced lateral root density.
evidence.claim: Auxin promotes LBD expression to drive lateral root formation; loss of TAA1/TAR2 reduces auxin and lateral root density.

edges_added.mechanism: Auxin is the master hormonal promoter of lateral root initiation, founder-cell specification, and primordium emergence.

evidence.title: Molecular mechanisms of lateral root formation: an emerging hologenomic perspective
evidence.evidence_sentence: Local auxin biosynthesis via TAA1/TAR and YUCCA enzymes, followed by polar transport through PINs, establishes the auxin maximum required for lateral root initiation and density.
evidence.claim: Auxin positively regulates lateral root density; taa1 tar2 auxin-biosynthesis mutants have reduced lateral root density.

edges_added.mechanism: BZR1 and BES1 (BZR2) are close paralogs with redundant function. They heterodimerize and bind many shared genomic targets; they behave as a composite BZR/BES transcriptional activator.

evidence.title: Brassinosteroid Signaling Network and Regulation of Photomorphogenesis
evidence.evidence_sentence: BZR1 and BES1/BZR2 are close paralogs acting redundantly; gain-of-function bzr1-1D phenocopies bes1-D, with constitutive BR responses including enhanced growth and elongation.
evidence.claim: BZR1 and BES1 are paralogous transcription factors with shared function; BZR1 activity drives the same cell-elongation program as the BZR_BES composite.

edges_added.mechanism: BIN2 phosphorylates and inactivates BZR1/BES1, blocking BR-responsive cell-elongation transcription. bin2-1 gain-of-function causes dwarfism.

evidence.title: BIN2, a New Brassinosteroid-Insensitive Locus in Arabidopsis
evidence.evidence_sentence: At maturity, the main inflorescence stem was about one-half of the wild-type height in bin2 gain-of-function mutants; BIN2 is a negative regulator of brassinosteroid signaling and plant height.
evidence.claim: BIN2 negatively regulates Plant_Height; bin2 gain-of-function is a semidwarf.

edges_added.mechanism: Active PHYB (high R:FR) represses BRC1 transcription in axillary buds; phyB loss-of-function or low R:FR derepresses BRC1 and enforces bud dormancy.

evidence.title: BRANCHED1 promotes axillary bud dormancy in response to shade in Arabidopsis
evidence.authors: Gonzalez-Grandio E; Poza-Carrion C; Sorzano COS; Cubas P
evidence.evidence_sentence: Active phyB down-regulates BRC1 in axillary buds under high R:FR; phyB mutants have elevated BRC1, increased bud dormancy, and fewer branches, phenocopying shade avoidance.
evidence.claim: PHYB represses BRC1; phyB KO upregulates BRC1 and reduces shoot branching.
